title: TC001-Load configuration successfully using a valid path
description: Verifies a user can change the config path, load it, and see a success indication and updated status.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//input[@placeholder="Config path" or @aria-label="Config path input field" or @name="configPath"]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Config loaded').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=~/mycar').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Config path input field not found on page
- Load button not found on page
- 'Config loaded' text not visible on page
- "~/mycar" text not visible on page
- Page rendered blank with 0 interactive elements after navigation to /

title: TC002-Show error when configuration load fails for an invalid path

title: TC003-Load button is actionable after editing the config path
description: Verifies the user can edit the config path field and initiate a load without UI preventing the action.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type '~/mycar' into the Config path input field (index 54) and then click the 'Load config' button (index 56), wait briefly, and check for the text 'Loading'.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/div[1]/div[1]/div[2]/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('~/mycar')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/div[1]/div[1]/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/div[1]/div[1]/div[2]/div/div/input').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Loading').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/div[1]/div[1]/div[2]/div/button').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC004-Whitespace-trimmed path input still loads successfully
description: Verifies leading/trailing spaces in the path do not prevent a successful config load (if supported by the UI/backend).
code:

        frame = context.pages[-1]

        # --> Assertions to verify final state

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

    finally:

            await pw.stop()

title: TC008-Load a valid tub from a relative path and verify metadata is shown
description: Verifies a user can enter a valid relative tub path, load the tub, and see record count and fields reflected in the UI.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type './data' into the Tub path input (index 123), click 'Load tub' (index 117), wait for the UI to update, then extract visible text to verify 'Loading', 'Success', 'record', and 'fields' appear.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/div[1]/div[2]/div[2]/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('./data')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/div[1]/div[2]/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/div[1]/div[2]/div[2]/div/div/input').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Loading').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Success').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=record').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=fields').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Load tub failed with error banner 'Error: Directory not found' displayed.
- Tub Navigator shows 'No records loaded' after attempting to load './data'.
- No 'Loading' or 'Success' messages appeared following the Load action.
- Tub path input contained './data' but the application reported the directory does not exist, preventing records/fields from being shown.

        # -> Type './data' into the Tub path input (index 69) then click the 'Load tub' button (index 71). After clicking, wait 2 seconds to allow any immediate loading feedback to appear and then check for the 'Loading' text and the 'Status bar' element.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/div[1]/div[2]/div[2]/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('./data')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/div[1]/div[2]/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Load tub' button at index 117, wait 2 seconds, then check whether the text 'Loading' appears and whether an element labeled or functioning as 'Status bar' is visible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/div[1]/div[2]/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Loading').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//div[contains(normalize-space(.), "Status bar") or @aria-label="Status bar" or contains(@class,"status") or contains(@id,"status")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

- Status bar element not present or visible after clicking 'Load tub'.

import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():

    browser = None

        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(

            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode

        await expect(frame.locator('text=record').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
